{{Quote|Language is an organism. The entity of language is born, germinates, hybrids itself, and through the earnestness of social and political forces, languages can even die.|Arner Figgis|<ref name="AJ7" />}}
'''Arner Figgis''' was an [[Issori]] linguist and anthropologist who advanced the controversial survival-regressive-isolationist theory, which alleged that colonists who survived a disaster might, if isolated from other colonists, be forced by necessity to abandon high technology and adopt characteristics of more primitive [[Human/Legends|Human]] cultures. One possible example of Figgis' theory was the [[Bharhulai]] tribe on [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]].

Arner Figgis served as the [[Chief Linguist]] for the [[University of Be'nal]] on [[Issor]] and achieved tenure at the [[Imperial Academy of Science and Methodology]] despite being non-human.

He travelled to [[Socorro/Legends|Socorro]] to study the [[Socorran (language)/Legends|Socorran language]] and its links to [[Olys Corellisi|Old Corellian]]. The scholar hired [[Trep Winterrs]] to help him. They travelled to the [[Black Dust Tavern]] so he could practice speaking the language with the [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]] [[Karl Mathieu Ancher|Karl Ancher]]. However, Figgis mispronounced a phrase and angered the bartender. Winterrs was able to smooth things over and invited Figgis to join him in a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabbaac]] so he could converse with the players.<ref name="AJ7" />
